product_overview: Saizu is a high-performance npm package and GitHub repository analyzer that provides install/gzip size, file type breakdown, dependency metrics, module format detection and download-time estimates via a REST API and SVG badges.
core_goals: Provide accurate, real-time analysis of npm packages (including scoped and versioned packages) and public GitHub repositories
Offer a simple REST API to retrieve analysis results and compare two targets programmatically
Expose small, embeddable SVG badges for quick size visibility
Deliver fast responses with in-memory caching and sensible defaults for performance and reliability
Gracefully handle errors and provide clear status/health information for automation and CI integrations

features.name: NPM Package Analysis
features.description: Analyze any published npm package (including scoped packages and specific versions) to return install size, gzip size, file-type breakdown, dependency list/count, module format support, types availability, license and download-time estimates.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/package/is-odd -> Receive 200 with JSON containing source:npm, packageName:is-odd, packageVersion, gzipSize, installSize, fileCount, typeBreakdown, dependencies, dependencyCount, hasESM, hasCJS, hasTypes, downloadTime, cachedAt
GET /api/v1/package/is-odd?version=1.0.0 -> Receive 200 with analysis JSON for requested version
GET /api/v1/package/@scope%2Fpackage -> Receive 200 with analysis JSON for scoped package
GET /api/v1/package/nonexistent-package -> Receive 404 with JSON error indicating package not found
GET /api/v1/package/react (large package) -> Receive 504 with JSON error indicating analysis timeout or resource limits

features.name: GitHub Repo Analysis
features.description: Clone and analyze a public GitHub repository (optionally a branch and subpath) to produce the same metrics as npm package analysis and, when applicable, compare to the published npm package.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/repo/facebook/react?branch=main&subpath=packages/core -> Receive 200 with JSON containing source:github, owner:facebook, repo:react, branch:main, subpath:packages/core, commit, packageName, packageVersion, gzipSize, installSize, fileCount, typeBreakdown, dependencies, dependencyCount, hasESM, hasCJS, hasTypes, downloadTime, npmComparison, cachedAt
GET /api/v1/repo/someuser/somerepo -> Receive 200 with analysis JSON for default branch
GET /api/v1/repo/unknown/unknown -> Receive 404 with JSON error indicating repository not found
GET /api/v1/repo/large/monorepo -> Receive 504 with JSON error indicating analysis timeout (git may be slow or unavailable)

features.name: Package Comparison
features.description: Compare two targets (npm package names or GitHub targets using github:owner/repo) side-by-side to return both analyses and a computed diff highlighting which target is smaller and numeric deltas for key metrics.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/compare?a=react&b=vue -> Receive 200 with JSON containing a (analysis object), b (analysis object), diff { gzipSize, installSize, dependencyCount, smaller }
GET /api/v1/compare?a=github:facebook/react&b=react -> Receive 200 with JSON comparing GitHub source to published npm package including npmComparison fields
GET /api/v1/compare?a=react -> Receive 400 with JSON error indicating missing required query parameter b
GET /api/v1/compare?a=nonexistent&b=vue -> Receive 404 with JSON error indicating target a not found or analysis failed

features.name: Badge Generation
features.description: Generate a small SVG badge for a package showing gzip or install size. Supports scoped packages via URL-encoding and returns image/svg+xml.
user_flows: GET /badge/ms?type=gzip -> Receive 200 with image/svg+xml badge showing gzip size
GET /badge/%40scope%2Fpackage?type=install -> Receive 200 with image/svg+xml badge for scoped package
GET /badge/ms (no type param) -> Receive 200 with default badge (e.g., gzip) as image/svg+xml
GET /badge/ms?type=unknown -> Receive 400 with JSON or text error indicating invalid badge type

features.name: Health Check
features.description: Expose a lightweight health endpoint returning service status, current version and uptime to be used by monitoring and orchestration systems.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/health -> Receive 200 with JSON { status: "ok", version: "<app version>", uptime: <seconds> }
POST /api/v1/health -> Receive 405 Method Not Allowed indicating only GET is supported
GET /api/v1/health when service is degraded -> Receive 503 with JSON error and diagnostic message (used for maintenance or degraded states)

routes.description: Health check endpoint. Returns JSON with status, version, uptime.
Analyze a published npm package by name. Supports scoped packages via wildcard routing. Optional query param: ?version=x.y.z

response_schema.typeBreakdown: object (keys are file extensions like "js", "ts", "json", values are byte counts)

routes.description: Analyze a public GitHub repository. Optional query params: ?branch=main&subpath=packages/core

response_schema.typeBreakdown: object (keys are file extensions, values are byte counts)
response_schema.npmComparison: object with keys available (boolean), publishedVersion, gzipDelta, installDelta, verdict

routes.description: Compare two npm packages or GitHub repos. Query params: ?a=react&b=vue Targets can be npm names or github:owner/repo format.
Generate an SVG badge for a package. Optional query param: ?type=gzip or ?type=install. For scoped packages use /badge/@scope/package. Returns SVG image with Content-Type image/svg+xml.

features.name: NPM Package Analysis
features.description: Analyzes install size, gzip size, file type breakdown, dependencies, module format and license of any npm package

features.name: GitHub Repo Analysis
features.description: Clones and analyzes a public GitHub repo to produce the same metrics as npm packages

features.name: Package Comparison
features.description: Compares two targets side-by-side using query params ?a=...&b=...

features.name: Badge Generation
features.description: Returns an SVG badge image showing gzip or install size for a package

features.name: Health Check
features.description: Simple health check returning status ok

known_limitations: The /api/v1/repo endpoint requires git to be installed and may timeout on very large repositories
Use small packages like "is-odd" or "ms" for testing npm analysis, not huge ones like "react"
Badge endpoint returns SVG, not JSON
